name: AXS Retest Hold Derisk v1
edge_mechanism: retest-and-hold continuation that de-risks by time if expansion fails to appear fast enough
thesis: AXS 1h is the best structurally different lane for testing whether a retest-and-hold continuation can survive a choppy backdrop if the trade explicitly de-risks by time when expected expansion does not arrive quickly. The edge is in disciplined early de-risking, not in forcing large trend targets.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest-and-hold entries remain too sparse, if time-based de-risking cannot offset failed expansions, or if the lane is too noisy to support orderly retests.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price retests reclaimed value after a small breakout, holds the retest, and either expands quickly or gets cut down by time-based de-risking before churn grows.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In choppy markets, continuation sometimes works only when the move proves itself fast. A time-based de-risk rule can preserve the handful of valid expansions while limiting the cost of stalled setups.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is expressive enough for retest-and-go behavior and should stress-test whether time-based de-risking adds real robustness.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Retests may occur often enough, but if genuine expansion rarely follows, even early de-risking will not create edge.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are dense but expectancy remains weak, tighten the retest quality filter before altering the time-based de-risk rule.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A reasonable number of retest entries with many quickly cut flat-to-small results and a smaller subset producing usable expansion.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 1h and one cleaner cross-lane show credible density with improved downside control from time-based de-risking.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse retests, or frequent stalled setups that still lose too much before de-risking.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest, hold, and timed de-risking are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad retest windows rather than exact breakout-candle perfection

validation_basket.why_included: Best stress-test lane for retest-hold continuation with time-based de-risking.
validation_basket.continue_if: Retests are frequent enough and the timed de-risk rule preserves expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Retests are too sparse or still decay before expansion.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: A mix of quick scratch-like exits and occasional meaningful expansion legs.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same de-risking logic survives on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves the retest-and-go shape with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The mechanism only works on high-beta noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but smaller retest continuations than AXS.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism transfers to the other major lane in the basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC retains the same retest-hold and rapid expansion pattern.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea does not generalize beyond AXS.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but more orderly retest continuations than AXS.

long: De-risk 20% after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.10 * ATR_14
Exit remaining 10% on close below EMA_20 or 4 bars
short: De-risk 20% after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.10 * ATR_14
Exit remaining 10% on close above EMA_20 or 4 bars
